member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions
member.summary: Tuning parameters for the . All properties expose sensible defaults suitable for 30-day wearable exports.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.Default
member.summary: Shared immutable default instance. Do not mutate; create a new instance when customisation is required.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.AnalysisWindowDays
member.summary: Number of calendar days to look back when building per-metric time series. Larger windows improve statistical confidence but may span device-change boundaries. Default: 30.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.SignificanceThreshold
P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.MinimumSampleCount
member.summary: Minimum number of aligned days required before a metric pair is computed. Pairs with fewer shared dates are silently skipped. Default: 7.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.IncludeWeakCorrelations
member.summary: When , correlations below are retained in the output. Useful for diagnostic purposes. Default: .

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.ComputeAllPairs
member.summary: When , all N×(N-1)/2 metric pairs extracted from the collection are analysed. When , only the engine's curated default pairs plus any entries in are computed. Default: .

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.EnableParallelComputation
member.summary: When , metric pairs are processed concurrently using . Default: .

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.MaxDegreeOfParallelism
member.summary: Maximum number of concurrent tasks used during parallel correlation computation. Has no effect when is . Default: 4.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.InsightMode
member.summary: Controls how many insights are emitted for correlations that are not in the curated pattern library. Default: .

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.AdditionalMetricPairs
member.summary: Extra metric pairs to analyse in addition to the defaults (or instead of them when is ).

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.MaxLagDays
member.summary: Maximum lag (in days) to explore when calling . Default: 7.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.Validate
member.summary: Validates the option values and returns any configuration errors.
member.returns: Enumeration of human-readable error messages; empty if valid.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions.IsValid
member.summary: Returns when no validation errors are present.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationEngine
member.summary: Default implementation of . Extracts per-metric time series from a , computes Pearson r for every metric pair, and maps significant correlations to a curated set of health insights with actionable recommendations.
member.remarks: Designed for singleton registration. All public methods are stateless and safe to call concurrently from multiple threads.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationEngine.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationEngine})
member.summary: Default implementation of . Extracts per-metric time series from a , computes Pearson r for every metric pair, and maps significant correlations to a curated set of health insights with actionable recommendations.
member.remarks: Designed for singleton registration. All public methods are stateless and safe to call concurrently from multiple threads.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationStrength
member.summary: Categorical strength of a Pearson correlation coefficient. Values are ordered so that ≥ comparisons work correctly.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationStrength.Negligible
member.summary: |r| < 0.10 — effectively random noise.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationStrength.Weak
member.summary: 0.10 ≤ |r| < 0.30 — small but present relationship.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationStrength.Moderate
member.summary: 0.30 ≤ |r| < 0.50 — meaningful, practically notable.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationStrength.Strong
member.summary: 0.50 ≤ |r| < 0.70 — robust, clearly present.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationStrength.VeryStrong
member.summary: |r| ≥ 0.70 — dominant linear relationship.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationDirection
member.summary: Sign of the correlation coefficient.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationDirection.Negative
member.summary: Metrics move in opposite directions.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationDirection.None
member.summary: No meaningful linear relationship detected.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationDirection.Positive
member.summary: Metrics move in the same direction.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.InsightSeverity
member.summary: Importance level of a cross-metric health insight. Ordered so that ≥ comparisons work correctly.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.InsightSeverity.Informational
member.summary: Interesting but does not require action.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.InsightSeverity.Moderate
member.summary: Pattern worth monitoring over the coming weeks.

member.name: F:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.InsightSeverity.Significant
member.summary: Clinically relevant pattern; actionable recommendation included.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationServiceExtensions

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationServiceExtensions.AddCorrelationEngine(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,System.Action{HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions})
member.summary: Registers as a singleton backed by .
member.param: Optional delegate to customise . When supplied, the configured instance is registered as a singleton so that application code can inject it directly if needed.
member.returns: The same instance for chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when the supplied options fail validation.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationServiceExtensions.AddCorrelationEngine(Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection.IServiceCollection,HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions)
member.summary: Registers using a pre-built instance.
member.returns: The same instance for chaining.
member.exception: Thrown when the supplied options fail validation.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.CorrelationServiceExtensions.GetCorrelationEngine(System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Resolves from the service provider. Helper for one-shot usage where full DI wiring is not required.
member.returns: The registered instance.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine
member.summary: Analyses relationships between health metrics extracted from a and surfaces cross-metric insights.
member.remarks: Implementations must be thread-safe; the default implementation () is safe for singleton registration.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine.AnalyzeAsync(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,HealthDataExportTools.Configuration.CorrelationEngineOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs a full cross-metric correlation analysis over the supplied collection and returns all computed pairs together with curated health insights.
member.param: Source health data. The engine reads sleep, heart-rate, SpO₂, and steps records.
Token to cancel long-running parallel computations.
member.returns: A containing all correlations and insights.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine.GetInsightsAsync(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,System.Int32,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Convenience method that returns only the insight layer from a full analysis, using a simplified single-parameter API.
member.param: Look-back window in calendar days. Default: 30.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine.ComputePearsonCorrelation(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Double},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Double})
member.summary: Computes the Pearson product-moment correlation coefficient between two equal-length value sequences.
member.param: First variable — must be same length as .
member.returns: r ∈ [-1, 1], or when inputs are too short or collinear.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine.ExtractTimeSeries(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,System.Int32)
member.summary: Builds per-metric from all data types in the collection, filtering to the most recent days and aggregating multiple records per day to a single scalar value.
member.returns: One per available metric.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine.AnalyzeLag(HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.MetricTimeSeries,HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.MetricTimeSeries,System.Int32)
member.summary: Explores temporal lead-lag relationships between two pre-extracted time series, shifting metric B by 0– days and computing the Pearson r at each offset.
member.returns: Results ordered by |r| descending, with lag=0 representing the same-day correlation.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Correlation.ICorrelationEngine.ComputeLaggedCorrelation(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Double},System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{System.Double},System.Int32)
member.summary: Computes the Pearson r between metric A and metric B shifted by . A positive lag tests whether A predicts B in the future.
member.param: Outcome values (metric B), same length as .
Days to shift forward relative to .
member.returns: Pearson r for the lagged pair, or if insufficient data.

member.summary: Categorical strength derived from the absolute coefficient.

member.summary: Sign of the relationship (positive, negative, or none).

member.summary: Number of date-aligned data points used in the computation.

member.summary: Plain-language summary of the coefficient, strength, and direction.

member.summary: Last day included in the analysis window for this pair.

member.summary: Absolute value of ; used for ranking.

member.summary: Number of days spanned by the analysis window for this pair.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Formatters.CsvFormatter
member.summary: Formats health data into CSV (Comma-Separated Values) format. Provides separate CSV files for each data type to maintain clean structure. This implementation hardens CSV output against locale‑dependent formatting and CSV injection attacks (OWASP). All numeric and date values are rendered using and ISO‑8601 date strings. Fields that start with '=', '+', '-', or '@' are prefixed with a single quote to prevent Excel formula injection. RFC 4180 quoting rules are delegated to CsvHelper.

member.summary: File extension for CSV files.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for the RetryHandler

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.MaxRetries
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum number of retry attempts. Default is 3.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.InitialDelayMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the initial delay in milliseconds before the first retry. Default is 100ms.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.MaxDelayMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum delay in milliseconds between retries. Default is 30000ms (30 seconds).

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.CircuitBreakerMaxBackoffMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum backoff time in milliseconds for the circuit breaker half-open state. Default is 60000ms (1 minute).

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.CircuitBreakerFailureThreshold
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of consecutive failures required to open the circuit breaker. Default is 5.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.CircuitBreakerSuccessThreshold
member.summary: Gets or sets the number of consecutive successes required to close the circuit breaker after being half-open. Default is 2.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.CircuitBreakerHalfOpenMinDelayMs
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum time in milliseconds between half-open probes. Default is 5000ms (5 seconds).

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.HonorRetryAfterHeader
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to honor the Retry-After header on 429 (Too Many Requests) and 503 (Service Unavailable) responses. When true, the actual delay will be the maximum of the Retry-After value and the calculated backoff delay. Default is true.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.UseDecorrelatedJitter
member.summary: Gets or sets whether to use decorrelated jitter for exponential backoff. Default is true. When true, uses decorrelated jitter algorithm for more evenly distributed retry attempts. When false, uses traditional exponential backoff with fixed multiplier.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.IdempotentHttpMethods
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of HTTP methods that are considered idempotent and safe to retry. Default includes GET, HEAD, OPTIONS, PUT, and DELETE.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.RetryableStatusCodes
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of HTTP status codes that should trigger a retry. Default includes 408 (Request Timeout), 429 (Too Many Requests), and 5xx (Server Errors).

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.RetryableExceptions
member.summary: Gets or sets the list of exception types that should trigger a retry. Default includes common transient exceptions.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.CircuitBreakerEnabled
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the circuit breaker is enabled. Default is true.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions.Validate
member.summary: Validates the configuration and throws if invalid.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler
member.summary: Retry handler for resilient operations with exponential backoff, Retry-After header support, and circuit breaker pattern

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.#ctor(Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILogger{HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler},HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandlerOptions)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the RetryHandler class.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.ExecuteAsync``1(System.String,System.Func{System.Threading.Tasks.Task{``0}},System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpMethod)
member.summary: Execute operation with retries on failure using decorrelated-jitter exponential backoff
member.typeparam: The return type of the operation.
member.param: Name of the operation for logging purposes.
Optional request URI for Retry-After header support.
Optional HTTP method for idempotency checking.
member.returns: The result of the operation if successful.
member.exception: Thrown when circuit breaker is open and not ready for half-open probe.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.Execute``1(System.String,System.Func{``0},System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpMethod)
member.summary: Execute operation with retries on failure using decorrelated-jitter exponential backoff
member.typeparam: The return type of the operation.
member.param: Name of the operation for logging purposes.
Optional request URI for Retry-After header support.
Optional HTTP method for idempotency checking.
member.returns: The result of the operation if successful.
member.exception: Thrown when operation is null or operationName is null/empty.
Thrown when circuit breaker is open and not ready for half-open probe.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.ShouldRetry(System.Exception,System.String,System.Net.Http.HttpMethod)
member.summary: Determines whether an exception should trigger a retry based on configuration.
member.param: Optional request URI for Retry-After header support.
Optional HTTP method for idempotency checking.
member.returns: True if the exception should trigger a retry; otherwise, false.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.CheckCircuitBreakerState
member.summary: Checks the circuit breaker state and transitions if necessary.
member.exception: Thrown when circuit breaker is open and not ready for half-open probe.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.CalculateDelay(System.Int32,System.Exception,System.String)
member.summary: Calculates the delay for the next retry attempt using decorrelated jitter exponential backoff.
member.param: The exception that triggered the retry.
Optional request URI for Retry-After header support.
member.returns: The delay duration before the next retry attempt.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Integration.RetryHandler.CalculateBaseDelay(System.Int32)
member.summary: Calculates the base delay using decorrelated jitter exponential backoff.
member.returns: The base delay duration.

member.summary: Extracts the Retry-After delay from response headers or exception data.

member.returns: The Retry-After delay if available; otherwise, null.

member.summary: Gets the file extension for CSV format.

member.summary: Gets a human-readable description of the CSV export format.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExporter.ExportToCsvStreamAsync(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,System.IO.TextWriter,HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Export all enabled data types from to a single CSV stream asynchronously. This streaming approach avoids building the entire payload in memory, making it suitable for large datasets.
member.param: The text writer to write to; must not be null.
Optional export options controlling which data types and columns to include, and the date format to use. When null, all data types are exported with ISO 8601 date formatting.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation.

member.name: T:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions
member.summary: Configuration options for CSV export, including column selection and date formatting.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.IncludeSleep
member.summary: Include sleep records in the export. Default: true.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.IncludeHeartRate
member.summary: Include heart rate records in the export. Default: true.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.IncludeSpO2
member.summary: Include SpO2 records in the export. Default: true.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.IncludeSteps
member.summary: Include steps records in the export. Default: true.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.IncludeActivity
member.summary: Include activity records in the export. Default: true.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.DateFormat
member.summary: Date format string used for all date columns. Defaults to ISO 8601 ("yyyy-MM-dd").

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.SleepColumns
member.summary: Subset of sleep column names to include. When null or empty, all columns are exported. Recognised names: Date, Duration, DeepSleep, LightSleep, REM, Awake, Quality, Score, AvgHeartRate.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.HeartRateColumns
member.summary: Subset of heart rate column names to include. When null or empty, all columns are exported. Recognised names: Date, MinBpm, MaxBpm, AvgBpm, RestingBpm, Measurements, StressLevel, CardioZone, Zone1Minutes, Zone2Minutes, Zone3Minutes, Zone4Minutes, Zone5Minutes.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.SpO2Columns
member.summary: Subset of SpO2 column names to include. When null or empty, all columns are exported. Recognised names: Date, MinPercentage, MaxPercentage, AvgPercentage, Measurements.

member.name: P:HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions.StepsColumns
member.summary: Subset of steps column names to include. When null or empty, all columns are exported. Recognised names: Date, Steps, DistanceKm, Calories, GoalAchievement, ActiveMinutes, Walking, Running.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Services.IHealthDataExporter.ExportToCsvAsync(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,System.String,HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions)
member.summary: Export all enabled data types from to separate CSV files inside .
member.param: Directory where the CSV files will be written.
Optional export options controlling which data types and columns to include, and the date format to use. When null, all data types are exported with ISO 8601 date formatting.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Services.IHealthDataExporter.ExportToCsvStreamAsync(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,System.IO.TextWriter,HealthDataExportTools.Services.CsvExportOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Export all enabled data types from to a single CSV stream asynchronously. This streaming approach avoids building the entire payload in memory, making it suitable for large datasets.
member.param: The text writer to write to; must not be null.
Optional export options controlling which data types and columns to include, and the date format to use. When null, all data types are exported with ISO 8601 date formatting.
member.returns: A task representing the asynchronous operation.

member.summary: Detects statistical anomalies and computes directional trends across all tracked health metrics using Z-score analysis over a configurable rolling window.

member.summary: Minimum data points required before trend or anomaly analysis is attempted.

member.summary: Initialise a new instance of .
member.param: Logger instance provided by the DI container.

member.name: M:HealthDataExportTools.Services.TrendAnomalyDetectionService.AnalyzeAsync(HealthDataExportTools.Services.HealthDataCollection,System.Int32,System.Double,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Run trend and anomaly analysis across all metrics in a . Each metric is processed concurrently and the results are collected into a single report.
member.param: Z-score magnitude at or above which a data point is flagged as anomalous (default 2.0).
Propagates notification that the operation should be cancelled.
member.returns: An summarising findings per metric.
member.exception: Thrown when an unexpected error occurs during analysis.

member.summary: Detect anomalies and classify the trend direction for an ordered time series. Exposed as public to allow direct unit testing of the core algorithm.
member.param: Display name used in the returned result.
Chronologically ordered (date, value) pairs within the analysis window.
Z-score magnitude at or above which a point is flagged.
member.returns: A describing trend direction and any detected anomalies.
